notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A close back below the $260.22 52w low on rising volume would confirm the downtrend is intact and flip this to a neutral/trim tactical stance.

positional.falsifier: Next earnings showing book-to-bill <0.95 with backlog YoY turning negative, or a further leg down in op margin below 9%, would confirm structural erosion and flip positional to neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that forward P/E is actually >23x AND above 115% of its own 3Y average (a genuine premium, not a trailing artifact), combined with DoD budget slipping to sequester_risk, would flip strategic firmly to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp single-session move (>3%) on order-book news or an RSI spike above 70/below 30 would create a genuine tactical signal in one direction.

positional.falsifier: A drawdown exceeding 15% from highs (flips to buy) or a broad TSMC+Samsung+Intel capex-cut cluster confirmed in guidance (flips to trim).

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E dropping below 25x on a >15% drawdown (flips to buy) or, conversely, forward P/E pushing above 45x with book-to-bill falling below 1.0 (would justify a stronger trim).

tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close below SMA200 ($472.61) on elevated volume, or RSI breaking below 30, would flip this to a trim/momentum-broken signal.

positional.falsifier: LIN/APD spread widening past +3pp (LIN becoming expensive vs peers) combined with BBB OAS breaking above ~130bps would flip this to a trim.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed >$1B H2 mega-project win or a genuine adjusted (not GAAP) ROCE/margin acceleration with backlog inflection would flip this to a net buy; conversely a documented structural ROCE decline below 22% on an ADJUSTED basis would deepen the trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the $349 52w low on rising volume would flip this to a momentum-breakdown SELL; conversely a reclaim of the 50d ($400) would strengthen the buy.

positional.falsifier: A reclaim and hold above the 200d ($437) would confirm the drawdown was a shakeout and justify max conviction; a broken Azure CC print (<22% cc growth) would flip the sign to trim by validating the downtrend as fundamental.

strategic.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth decelerating to <22% with capex still rising (ROI question) would flip strategic to trim; conversely an Azure re-acceleration above 30% would justify max strategic conviction.

tactical.falsifier: RSI spiking above 72 with price stalling at/above the $58.41 52w high (flip positive), or a sharp gap-down to the $52-53 200-day area on a rate shock (flip negative).

positional.falsifier: Yield spread compressing further below ~0bps (10Y rising above ENB's yield) alongside a break below the 50-day would confirm the trim (push more positive); conversely a 10Y drop toward 4.2% widening the spread past ~75-100bps would flip me toward buy.

strategic.falsifier: A dividend-growth guidance cut, a distributable-cash-flow coverage deterioration, or a major Mainline/regulatory setback would flip this decisively positive; a decisive dovish BoC/Fed pivot pushing the 10Y toward 4% and re-rating yield names would flip it negative.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close above the 50-day SMA ($14.81) on rising volume would flip this to a modest buy; conversely a close below the 200-day ($12.82) would flip it to a clear trim.

positional.falsifier: GLNCY/COPX spread turning materially negative (GLNCY lagging copper by >1pp) plus a break of the 200-day would flip this positive/trim; a spread turning strongly positive with a 50-day reclaim would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained LME copper break above ~$5/lb with drawing inventories would deepen the buy; conversely a collapse in copper below ~$3.80 combined with China PMI back into contraction and coal prices rolling over would flip the strategic case to trim.

tactical.falsifier: RSI dropping below 30 on a capitulation flush (flip more negative/buy) OR funding rates spiking >0.05% for 7+ days with price re-extending (flip positive/trim).

positional.falsifier: A confirmed reclaim of the 50DMA ($38.16) on rising net ETF inflows would validate the buy; conversely a decisive break of the $32.84 52w low on accelerating outflows would flip me to neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Sustained monthly ETF outflows over a full quarter alongside failure to reclaim the 200DMA would flip strategic to a clear trim; conversely a durable inflow regime plus rate-cut cycle would push it firmly negative.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive break and hold above the 50-day SMA ($106) with expanding volume would flip me to a mild negative (buy) tactical read.

positional.falsifier: A sustained MXN move weaker than ~18.5/USD or a break below the 200-day SMA ($99.31) would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.falsifier: A regime shift to a persistently weak/volatile peso (MXN sustained >19/USD) or a margin-compressing shock from input costs/regulation would flip strategic positive.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close above the SMA50 ($18.09) on rising volume would flip this to a buy/continuation signal rather than a fade.

positional.falsifier: A sustained WTI break below $70 or a government announcement forcing dividend cuts / mandated capex would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.falsifier: Explicit government action cutting the dividend formula or mandating below-market fuel pricing (or a structural oil price collapse below pre-salt-plus-tax economics) would flip this to a clear trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sustained 2-week directional break in Drewry WCI (>15% move either way) accompanied by RSI moving outside 40-60 would flip this from neutral.

positional.falsifier: AMKBY breaking below its 200-day ($11.68) or GSCPI dropping below 0 would flip this to a buy-side (negative) positional read.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that carriers are permanently returning to Suez AND orderbook deliveries accelerating faster than scrapping — signaling structural overcapacity — would flip this positive.

tactical.falsifier: TMO breaking back below SMA50 ($487) on rising volume, or XBI rolling over -5%+ over a week, would flip this to a trim/neutral read.

positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling negative (funding re-freeze) or TMO decisively losing its relative lead vs DHR would flip the positional sign.

strategic.falsifier: Simultaneous China capex collapse and NIH funding cuts, or forward P/E pushing >28x on decelerating growth, would flip strategic to trim.

tactical.falsifier: Two consecutive up days on rising volume with RSI reclaiming 50 and price back above the SMA50 ($104) would confirm the bottom and push tactical more negative; conversely a break below the 52w low of $81.24 would flip me toward neutral/positive.

positional.falsifier: A cRPO disclosure confirming deceleration below 18% YoY, or EPS estimate revisions turning sharply negative over the next 30d, would validate the drawdown as fundamental and flip my positional score toward neutral/positive.

strategic.falsifier: A forward guidance cut or cRPO deceleration to <18% at next earnings would confirm the de-rating is structural and flip my strategic score positive; conversely cRPO re-acceleration would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A close below the 50d SMA ($505.82) on expanding volume, or drawdown deepening past -12%, would flip this to a tactical buy (negative).

positional.falsifier: Two consecutive quarters of negative EPS revisions or cross-border volume printing <6% would flip positional to trim (positive).

strategic.falsifier: Measurable real-economy merchant stablecoin displacement in disclosures, OR interchange/network-fee legislation moving to 'passed', would flip strategic to trim (positive).

tactical.falsifier: July 16 print shows procedures below 12% AND explicit US procedure-share loss to Hugo, or the 2027 transition quantified as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue) — that would flip this from oversold-buy to justified-selling.

positional.falsifier: 90d EPS revisions turning sharply negative alongside a procedure-guide cut on July 16 would confirm the downtrend is fundamentally justified and flip positional toward neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Measurable US robotic-surgery share loss to Hugo/Ottava, OR the 2027 transition quantified as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue), would break the strategic moat thesis and flip the score positive.
